opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My back is completely locked up, and the exhaustion is crushing me.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The pain flared up badly tonight and I can't bend or walk to the bathroom without it being agony. The paracetamol isn't touching it and I don't want to wait until morning.

symptom_narrative.onset: the lower back discomfort came on gradually over about 6 months, but it flared really badly tonight
symptom_narrative.duration: about 6 months of niggling pain, then it got much worse over the last 12 hours
symptom_narrative.character: a deep stiffness and locking, worst thing is how stiff I am, especially in the mornings
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: tonight it's severe, I can barely move
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: low in my back, and I get pain in my heels too
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: very stiff and sore in the mornings, eases a little once I get going, but tonight it just won't ease
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: moving around gently used to help a bit, and the mornings are the worst
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: resting and staying still seems to make the stiffness worse, and tonight everything makes it worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: crushing tiredness, and sore heels where the tendon joins
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had grumbling back stiffness for about half a year but never a flare this bad
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm stuck in bed right now, I can't walk to the bathroom without severe pain

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: the usual tablets from the packet

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I live on my own, and tonight I'm worried about falling — there are slippery rugs in the hallway and it's a long way to the bathroom

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a home thermometer
may be elevated by pain and distress

objective_data_offered.value: lying flat, moving very guardedly, looks distressed
